solution:  
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string greatestLetter(string s) {
        std::unordered_set<char> lower;
        std::unordered_set<char> upper;
        for (char c : s) {
            if (islower(c)) lower.insert(c);
            else if (isupper(c)) upper.insert(c);
        }

        for (char c = 'Z'; c >= 'A'; --c) {
            if (lower.find(tolower(c)) != lower.end() && upper.find(c) != upper.end()) {
                return string(1, c);
            }
        }

        return "";
    }
};
```  
efficiency: HIGH  
time complexity: O(n)  
space complexity: O(1)  
energy implications: LOW  
explanation: The solution uses two sets to track the presence of lowercase and uppercase letters. It then iterates from 'Z' to 'A' to find the greatest letter that exists in both sets. This ensures the highest possible letter is returned efficiently. The use of sets ensures constant-time lookups, and the iteration is bounded by the fixed size of the alphabet.
